steps.ordered: Ultrasound Abdomen (LIVER OR GALLBLADDER US (SINGLE ORGAN))
CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

study_inadequacy.evidence: The pancreas is partially obscured by bowel gas, but visualized portion of the pancreas is unremarkable.

alarm_detect.summary: The abdominal ultrasound did not fully visualize the pancreas due to overlying bowel gas, leaving the pancreatic question unanswered despite RUQ/epigastric pain in the workup.

alarm_resolve.reason: CT abdomen and pelvis with contrast will fully visualize the pancreas (overcoming the bowel-gas limitation of ultrasound) as well as the broader abdomen, directly resolving the study inadequacy flagged for incomplete pancreatic assessment.
